data.title: Americans are avoiding returns on broken or unwanted products due to difficult processes, with 46% forgoing returns in the past year.
data.description: In ADA, Oklahoma, a recent survey has revealed that American consumers are choosing to keep defective or unwanted purchases rather than returning them, citing a cumbersome return process as the main reason. Nearly 46% of consumers have held onto items in the past year, leading to a loss of entitled refunds. The trend is especially prevalent during the January return period, resulting in retailers retaining revenue from flawed products and buyer dissatisfaction.

data.title: Plymouth Industrial REIT stockholders approve all-cash acquisition by Makarora.
data.description: Today, Plymouth Industrial REIT, Inc. disclosed that its shareholders have given the green light for the all-cash acquisition of the company.

data.title: OceanFirst Financial Corp. reported lower net income available to common stockholders of $13.1M for Q4 2025, compared to $20.9M last year. Annual net income decreased to $67.1M in 2025 from $96M in 2024.
data.description: Red Bank, New Jersey - OceanFirst Financial Corp. (NASDAQ: OCFC), the parent company of OceanFirst Bank N.A., revealed that for the quarter concluding on December 31, 2025, they recorded a net income of $13.1 million available to common stockholders, equating to $0.23 per diluted share. This marks a decrease from the same period in the previous year which saw $20.9 million, or $0.36 per diluted share. The net income for the linked quarter was reported at $17.3 million, or $0.30 per diluted share. In terms of the annual performance up till December 31, 2025, the company disclosed a net income available to common stockholders of $67.1 million, translating to $1.17 per diluted share. This displayed a decline from the prior year's figures, which stood at $96.0 million, or $1.65 per diluted share. Further detailed performance metrics can be found in the "Selected Quarterly Financial Data" section.

data.title: Capital One stock falls on Q4 earnings miss, acquiring Brex for $5.15 billion with 50% cash and 50% stock.
data.description: Shares of Capital One Financial Corp. (NYSE: COF) fell in after-hours trading following the release of its fourth-quarter earnings report, which fell short of expectations. The company announced a deal to purchase fintech firm Brex Inc. for $5.15 billion, with half of the payment in cash and the other half in stock. Capital One disclosed key numbers from the quarter, with the full report accessible on Benzinga.com. Keep an eye on COF stock for further price movements.

data.title: Invitation Homes (NYSE: INVH) announces tax treatment of 2025 dividends for Common Stock. Details in table below.
data.description: Invitation Homes Inc. (NYSE: INVH) shared the tax treatment of its 2025 dividends for Common Stock. The company's tax return for 2025 is pending, so the details provided below are based on available information as of the release date:- Total dividends per share: $1.50- Ordinary dividends: $1.20- Capital gain distribution: $0.30It is essential to recognize that federal tax laws impact individuals uniquely, and therefore, no specific advice can be given by the company.

data.description: Rye, New York - Acadia Realty Trust (NYSE: AKR) ("Acadia" or the "Company") has announced the Federal tax treatment for 2025 distributions to holders of its common shares of beneficial interest (CUSIP #004239109) traded on the NYSE under the ticker symbol "AKR." The distribution record date, payment date, total distribution per share, total income, and various tax boxes are as follows: Record Date: [date], Payment Date: [date], Total Distribution Per Share: $[amount], Total Income: $[amount], Box 1a-Ordinary: $[amount], Box 1b-Qualified: $[amount], Box 5a-199A: $[amount], Box 2a-Capital Gain: $[amount], Box 2b-Unrecaptured Section 1250: $[amount], Box 2f-Section 897: $[amount].

data.title: BitGo's crypto IPO rises 20%, signaling potential for tech giants like SpaceX, OpenAI to consider public offerings.
data.description: BitGo's initial public offering (IPO) has started a promising year for tech companies, with potential public offerings from SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic on the horizon. BitGo's IPO saw a significant 20% surge in its stock price, indicating a positive outlook for upcoming public launches in the tech industry. This suggests a trend of increasing interest in public offerings among companies in the sector.

data.title: Gold prices surged due to Trump's Greenland threats, continue rising despite easing tensions. Potential for further increase.
data.description: The price of gold saw a significant increase in response to President Trump's threats regarding Greenland, but has continued to rise as tensions have begun to ease. Despite this short-term fluctuation, there are compelling long-term factors supporting a bullish outlook on gold.

data.title: Intuitive Surgical's stock rose after surpassing Q4 profit expectations, following a disappointing preannouncement.
data.description: Intuitive Surgical shares surged in after-hours trading on Thursday after reporting a stronger-than-anticipated fourth-quarter profit, which came as a pleasant surprise following a previously disappointing preannouncement. The Robotics company's stock saw a significant increase after it beat expectations for its earnings in Q4.
